full10k_questions.1_mcvr_vs_mmff94s_composite_geometry: Yes. V8 has lower weighted BAC (-0.20140865 vs 0.056056931), lower angle/ring deltas, and lower RMSD (1.3302337 vs 1.3823086).
full10k_questions.2_mcvr_faster_than_gfn2_xtb: Yes on observed wall time: V8 was 12.70x faster per record than the two-worker xTB run. Hardware differs, so this is an operational comparison, not algorithm-normalized CPU timing.
full10k_questions.3_xtb_physical_optimization_and_movement: GFN2-xTB lowered its own native energy on successful records and moved atoms much more (0.27327426 A vs V8 0.0034744402 A). Native xTB energy is not compared numerically with MMFF or neural methods.
full10k_questions.4_mmff_xtb_effect_on_rmsd_mat_cov_divers: MMFF worsened RMSD versus Raw (1.3823086 vs 1.330059) and increased duplicate rate to 0.006; xTB RMSD is 1.2918229, with diversity 2.3770978 and duplicate rate 0.0042.
full10k_questions.5_mcvr_small_movement_global_conformatio: Yes. V8 mean displacement is 0.0034744402 A while MAT-P/MAT-R are 1.3302337/2.0556156 and diversity remains 2.3882748.
full10k_questions.6_failure_and_coverage: MMFF success/fallback = 9951/10000 and 49/10000; xTB = 9989/10000 and 11/10000. No record was dropped.
full10k_questions.7_clash_power: Only 20 natural records were applicable for active clash, so clash inference is explicitly treated as low-power when its CI crosses zero.
full10k_questions.8_mcvr_gain_concentration: Yes. V8's main unified-evaluator gains remain weighted BAC (-0.20140865), angle (-0.0044310814), and ring (-0.019807461), with only 0.0034744402 A mean movement.
